rows.reviewReason: The supplied 'OLD' and 'NEW' texts for Item 4 both contain extensive content from Item 5 (Initial Fees), indicating a serious input segmentation error. Item 4 should contain only bankruptcy-related disclosures (e.g., franchisor or affiliate bankruptcies), but none appear. This invalidates comparative analysis of Item 4 itself.

rows.summary: Identical disclosure: both OLD and NEW state 'No bankruptcies are required to be disclosed in this Item' with no variation in substance, formatting, or content for Item 4.

The NEW Item states no bankruptcy is required to be disclosed — a complete deletion of the previously reported historical bankruptcy-related affiliation.

oldEvidence.quote: Our Chief Administrative Officer, Greg Buchanan, was a principal officer of Buffet Partners, L.P. (a/k/a Furr’s Restaurants, whose address and principal place of business is 120 Chula Vista Drive, San Antonio, Texas 78232).

Within one year after Mr. Buchanan left Furr’s Restaurants, that company filed as a debtor a voluntary petition for relief under Chapter 11 of the Bankruptcy Code in the U.S. Bankruptcy Court for the Northern District of Texas on February 4, 2014 (lead case # 14-30699-HDH-11), which court granted a motion for a structured dismissal of that Chapter 11 case in July, 2014.

newEvidence.quote: No bankruptcy is required to be disclosed in this Item.

rows.summary: The NEW Item 4 introduces a new state-specific deferral requirement for Illinois, mandating fee deferral until franchisor obligations are complete and franchisee opens, imposed by the Illinois Attorney General’s Office due to franchisor’s financial condition. All other content—including California, Maryland, Virginia deferrals; initial fee amount and terms; grand opening fee; delayed opening fee; Multi-Unit Operator Development Fee structure—is identical in substance and wording (allowing for minor OCR/typo variations). No change to bankruptcy disclosure itself—still states 'No bankruptcy information is required'.
rows.statedReason: This deferral has been imposed by the Illinois Attorney General’s Office based on the Franchisor’s financial condition

newEvidence.quote: In the State of Illinois, all fees are deferred until all initial obligations owed the Franchisee by the Franchisor or affiliates have been completed and the Franchisee has opened for business
This deferral has been imposed by the Illinois Attorney General’s Office based on the Franchisor’s financial condition

rows.summary: Item 4 (Bankruptcy) is identical in both versions: a single sentence stating 'No bankruptcies are required to be disclosed in this Item', followed by a blank-page notice. No wording, formatting, punctuation, or structural variation is present between OLD [[PAGE 0015]] and NEW [[PAGE 0011]]. All other text differences occur in unrelated Items (e.g., Item 5, Item 6, Item 17), not Item 4.

The fee structure for the Initial Franchise Fee was fundamentally restructured from a per-household incremental model to a tiered flat-fee model (Level 1–3), increasing minimum entry cost and altering pricing logic. Additionally, NEW introduces a new security interest grant in Item 4 (via Note 8 referencing Franchise Agreement Section 22) — a material contractual obligation absent in OLD — securing all franchisee debts with franchise assets.

oldEvidence.quote:
The Initial Franchise Fee is a minimum amount of $30,000 for the initial 50,000 households or less  Additional households may be bought using the following fee schedule

From 50,001 households to 70,000 households, you will pay $0 55 per household
No bankruptcy information is required to be disclosed in this item

newEvidence.quote:
Level 1 = $30,000 for 50,000 (or less) households
Level 2 = $40,000 for 75,000 (or less) households (but more than 50,000 households)
Level 3 = $50,000 for 100,000 (or less) households (but more than 75,000 households)

Note 8  Under the Franchise Agreement Section 22, you grant to us a security interest in  all furniture, fixtures, equipment, signage and realty of your franchise operations to secure payment of all amounts due under the Franchise Agreement or otherwise by you, amounts we advance to protect the collateral, collection costs and all your indebtedness to us or third parties
